title: Compliance Shock Readiness

content.statement: Regulation is not just legal overhead; it is operational survivability.
content.question: What regulation in your industry or province could change and directly hurt your business model? What's your early-warning system for catching it?
content.equation: CSR = (Monitoring × Interpretation × Adaptation × Flexibility) ÷ Regulatory Exposure
sop: List every regulation that materially affects your business (licensing, labor, tax, industry-specific rules, data or privacy law).
Rate your Monitoring, Interpretation, Adaptation, and Flexibility 1-10 for how well you'd catch and respond to a change in each (see calculation, page 5).
Rate your Regulatory Exposure 1-10 for how much a change in that area would hurt you (see calculation, page 5).
Divide your readiness score by your exposure score to find your weakest area (see calculation, page 5), the lowest score is where a shock would hit hardest.
Set up one monitoring source (industry association, legal newsletter, regulator alert list) for your weakest area this week.
content.explainer: Regulation rarely ends a business. Unpreparedness for regulation does.
questions: What regulation in your industry or province could change and directly hurt your business model? What's your early-warning system for catching it?
Who on your team is actually responsible for tracking regulatory change, or is it nobody's job right now?
The last time a rule changed in your industry, how many days passed before you found out?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Monitoring 7, Interpretation 6, Adaptation 8, Flexibility 7. Multiplied: 7 times 6 times 8 times 7 equals 2,352, divided by Regulatory Exposure 5 equals 470.4. Higher scores mean you could absorb a regulatory shock without major disruption; lower scores mean a rule change could stop your business cold.
content.closing_statement: Regulatory readiness is a habit, not a scramble.

caption:
Principle Nº 49: Compliance Shock Readiness

Regulation is not just legal overhead; it is operational survivability. Regulation rarely ends a business.

Ask yourself: What regulation in your industry or province could change and directly hurt your business model? What's your early-warning system for catching it?
